# PR Status Monitor

> VS Code extension that displays real-time GitHub pull request CI status in the editor's status bar.

## Overview

PR Status Monitor is a Visual Studio Code extension that tracks open pull requests authored by the user across all repositories and Git worktrees in the workspace. It polls GitHub APIs (Actions check runs + commit statuses) and shows an aggregated pass/fail/pending summary in the VS Code status bar. It sends desktop notifications when PR statuses change and supports one-click navigation to PRs in the browser.

## Links

- Marketplace: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ah584d.pr-status-monitor
- Source: https://github.com/ah584d/vscode-plugin-pr-status
- Issues: https://github.com/ah584d/vscode-plugin-pr-status/issues

## Tech Stack

- Language: TypeScript
- Runtime: VS Code Extension Host (Node.js)
- Build: esbuild
- Package Manager: pnpm
- Linting: ESLint (flat config)
- Testing: @vscode/test-cli + Mocha
- GitHub API: @octokit/rest
- Authentication: VS Code built-in GitHub auth provider (no PAT required)

## Project Structure

- src/extension.ts: Main entry point — activation, polling loop, status bar, notifications
- src/types.ts: TypeScript interfaces for PRs, GitHub API responses, Octokit
- src/utils.ts: Pure utility functions — repo extraction, status determination, text formatting
- src/test/: Unit and integration tests
- assets/: Extension icon and screenshots
- package.json: Extension manifest (contributes commands, configuration, activation events)

## Key Concepts

- Polls GitHub using Octokit search API to find user-authored open PRs
- Determines CI status by combining check runs and commit statuses per PR
- Status hierarchy: failure > pending > success
- Uses a 10-second fast-poll on startup until first successful connection, then switches to configurable interval (default 2 min)
- Tracks previous PR statuses in a Map to detect transitions and fire notifications
- Supports multi-root workspaces and Git worktrees via VS Code's built-in Git extension API

## Configuration

- `prStatusMonitor.pollingInterval` (number, 1–60, default 2): Polling interval in minutes

## Contributing

1. Clone the repo
2. Run `pnpm install`
3. Press F5 in VS Code to launch the Extension Development Host
4. Run tests: `pnpm test`
5. Lint: `pnpm lint`
6. Package: `pnpm build`
